title: Autumn Durald Arkapaw Makes Oscar History for Best Cinematography - Variety
text: Autumn Durald Arkapaw has become the first woman to win the Academy Award for Best Cinematography, earning the honor for her work on the film Sinners. She is also the first woman of color to receive this recognition. The cinematographer, who is of Filipino and African American Creole descent, achieved another first by becoming the initial female cinematographer to shoot on both Imax 65mm and Ultra Panavision formats for Sinners. Prior to Arkapaw's win, only three women had ever received nominations in this category: Rachel Morrison in 2018 for Mudbound, Ari Wegner in 2021 for The Power of the Dog, and Mandy Walker in 2022 for Elvis. During her acceptance speech, Arkapaw thanked director Ryan Coogler and fellow cinematographer Rachel Morrison. She emphasized the collective effort behind her achievement, stating: moments like this don't happen without women standing up for you and advocating for you. Sinners achieved unprecedented success at the Oscars with 16 nominations total. Arkapaw's second collaboration with Coogler showcased her technical mastery, particularly in the film's notable piercing the veil sequence, which transitions between realistic and surreal cinematography.
